summary: Matched `fn_8031EFE4` in `src/melee/vi/vi0801.c` and updated its header prototype from unknown return/params to `void fn_8031EFE4(HSD_GObj*)`. The function uses baselib CObj/AObj types to animate a camera object, trigger `vi_8031C9B4` at specific frames, and call `lb_800145F4` plus `gm_801A4B60` at animation end. Bot report records one new 100% match for the function and `.sdata2` improvement from 0% to 38.89%.

key_files.role: Replaced `/// #fn_8031EFE4` stub with a typed C implementation and added required gm, lb, and baselib includes.
Updated `fn_8031EFE4` declaration from `UNK_RET fn_8031EFE4(UNK_PARAMS)` to `void fn_8031EFE4(HSD_GObj*)` and added baselib forward declarations.

what_changed.text: The implementation obtains `HSD_CObj* cobj = GET_COBJ(gobj)`, calls `HSD_CObjAnim(cobj)`, and checks `cobj->aobj->curr_frame` against `1.0F`, `30.0F`, `60.0F`, and `end_frame`.
At frames `1.0F` and `30.0F`, the function calls `vi_8031C9B4(0xC, 0)`; at frame `60.0F`, it calls `vi_8031C9B4(0x10, 0)`; at end frame, it calls `lb_800145F4()` and `gm_801A4B60()`.
Header prototype was made truthful enough for the recovered body by using `void` return and `HSD_GObj*` parameter.
Automated report recorded `fn_8031EFE4` as a new 100% match worth +152 bytes and reported `.sdata2` progress for `main/melee/vi/vi0801` from 0.00% to 38.89%.

smart_moves.text: Used real baselib camera/object types and fields (`HSD_GObj`, `HSD_CObj`, `aobj->curr_frame`, `aobj->end_frame`) instead of leaving unknown params or raw offset access.
Updated the public header prototype alongside the matched body, avoiding a lingering `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declaration.
Kept the implementation compact around frame-based camera animation side effects without introducing extra globals or data anchors in the diff.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body proves a concrete object type, update the owning header prototype in the same change so call surfaces no longer use `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`.
For camera-animation callbacks, typed baselib access through `GET_COBJ(gobj)` and `cobj->aobj` fields can express frame-gated side effects cleanly and match without raw pointer math in this PR.

naming_conventions.text: The function retained its address-style name `fn_8031EFE4`; the PR did not introduce a speculative semantic rename.
Local variables use short role names `gobj` and `cobj` for clear baselib object roles.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Frame comparisons were written directly as float literals (`1.0F`, `30.0F`, `60.0F`) and as an `end_frame` field comparison, with no evidence of artificial data-symbol promotion.
The match used normal C control flow with separate `if` checks for frame-triggered actions; no pragmas, register steering, inline assembly, or padding tactics appear in the diff.
Automated bot evidence reports the narrow new function match for `main/melee/vi/vi0801` / `fn_8031EFE4` at 100%.

follow_up_queries: vi0801 GET_COBJ camera animation frame triggers

notes: The diff is small and complete for the changed files, and the bot report directly supports the claimed 100% match for `fn_8031EFE4`.
No human review comments are present in the supplied slice, so `review_feedback` is empty.
The PR body claims `.sdata2` progress and the bot report repeats it, but the diff excerpt shows only source/header edits; no broader data-ownership conclusion is inferred.
No local build logs beyond the decomp-dev bot report are supplied.
